topic.what: Graph-to-model translation workflow for symbolic verification of post-quantum migration scenarios using Tamarin Prover
topic.how: Distributed communication graphs are canonically normalized and converted to .spthy models checking executability, secrecy, authentication, and forward secrecy under Dolev-Yao adversary
topic.why_matters: Post-quantum migration introduces protocol-level risks not observable through operational testing; symbolic verification provides complementary assurance layer for deployment topology analysis

characteristics.what_it_measures: Wall-clock time to complete symbolic verification
characteristics.context: Multi-hop S4 scenario is 8-10x slower than direct path scenarios due to complex symbolic search

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to produce differentiated outcomes vs uniform failure
characteristics.evidence: Table II shows 4 verified lemmas in S0, mixed outcomes in S1-S4, different falsification patterns per scenario
characteristics.context: Framework successfully distinguishes security outcomes rather than uniformly failing

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of scenarios passing executability sanity checks
characteristics.evidence: Table II: all 5 scenarios (S0-S4) show Exec. = verified
characteristics.context: Generated models are symbolically runnable across all evaluated topologies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Scenarios where secrecy lemma is falsified
characteristics.evidence: Table II: S1,S2,S3 falsify secrecy; S4 verifies secrecy
characteristics.context: Classical and asymmetric PQ paths fail secrecy; strong PQ multi-hop preserves it

characteristics.what_it_measures: Scenarios where authentication lemma is falsified
characteristics.evidence: Table II: only S4 falsifies authentication; S1-S3 verify authentication
characteristics.context: Stronger PQ posture paradoxically breaks authentication in multi-hop topology

characteristics.what_itasures: Mismatch between operational runtime status and symbolic verification outcome
characteristics.evidence: Table III: all S1-S4 show PASS at runtime but falsified lemmas under symbolic analysis
characteristics.context: Runtime correctness does not predict symbolic security properties
